Low price, wide outlet coverage and functional packaging fit a mass-market product.
The claims on the box support product communication.
This is coordinated marketing mix design for everyday buyers.

questions.question: Which statements are true about selling concept versus marketing concept? 1. Selling concept starts after production. 2. Marketing concept starts with consumer needs. 3. Selling concept focuses more on persuasion. 4. Marketing concept ignores customer satisfaction.
questions.explanation:
Selling concept usually follows production and relies on persuasion.
Marketing concept begins with needs and aims at satisfaction.
So statement 4 is false.

questions.question: Which of the following is most closely related to a warranty?
questions.explanation:
Warranty is a product-related assurance given to customers.
It enhances confidence and supports purchase decisions.
It is not a distribution or ad decision directly.

questions.question: A laptop brand offers a free bag, one-year support and a clear label on the box. Which product mix idea is being used?
questions.explanation:
The free bag, support and label add value beyond the laptop itself.
This is the augmented product concept.
It helps customers perceive more utility and service.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT normally a function of advertising?
questions.explanation:
Advertising communicates; it does not move goods physically.
Transport is a distribution function.
The other three are classic communication roles of advertising.

questions.question: A cosmetics company wants to change its image from ordinary to premium. It upgrades packaging, reduces discounting and shifts to selected stores. What is the most likely goal?
questions.explanation:
Packaging, pricing and distribution are all being adjusted to change market perception.
This is a repositioning effort toward premium image.
The firm is using the marketing mix to alter brand meaning.

questions.question: Assertion (A): Distribution channels can create place utility. Reason (R): They make products available where consumers need them, reducing search and travel effort.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation:
Place utility is created when products become available in convenient locations.
Channels do this by reducing the effort customers must spend to obtain goods.
So the reason explains the assertion.

questions.question: Which statements are true about product branding and promotion? 1. A strong brand can make promotion easier. 2. Promotional messages can reinforce brand identity. 3. Branding and promotion are unrelated. 4. Both can influence customer loyalty.
questions.explanation:
Branding and promotion support each other.
Promotional messages often strengthen identity and loyalty.
Statement 3 is false because the two are closely related.

questions.question: A factory makes a durable mixer grinder. Because buyers compare features carefully, the firm provides demonstrations, a warranty and selected dealers. Which marketing logic is most accurate?
questions.explanation:
Durable consumer goods are often shopping products because buyers compare features and prices.
Demonstrations and warranty support such comparison-based buying.
Selected dealers also help in service and availability.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a coupon?
questions.explanation:
A coupon gives buyers a direct incentive, usually a discount.
It is used to stimulate trial or repeat purchase.
This makes it a sales promotion tool.

questions.question: A brand of breakfast cereal uses a bright pack, a fair price, wide retail distribution and a morning TV campaign. Which statement best describes the strategy?
options: The firm is focusing only on one P
The firm is coordinating the marketing mix for mass appeal
The firm is using only personal selling
The firm is ignoring product differentiation
questions.explanation:
Bright packaging, price, availability and advertising are all working together.
That is coordinated marketing mix planning for a mass consumer product.
The result is stronger reach, visibility and customer appeal.
